root_text.sn50.55-66:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gaṅgā nadī pācīnaninnā pācīnapoṇā pācīnapabbhārā;
root_text.sn50.55-66:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañca balāni bhāvento pañca balāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro.
root_text.sn50.55-66:1.3: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañca balāni bhāvento pañca balāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro?
root_text.sn50.55-66:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu, saddhābalaṁ bhāveti rāgavinayapariyosānaṁ dosavinayapariyosānaṁ mohavinayapariyosānaṁ …
root_text.sn50.55-66:1.5: evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañca balāni bhāvento pañca balāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro”ti.

translation_text.sn50.55-66:0.2: 6. Another Chapter of Abbreviated Texts on the Ganges
translation_text.sn50.55-66:1.1: “Mendicants, the Ganges river slants, slopes, and inclines to the east.
translation_text.sn50.55-66:1.2: In the same way, a mendicant who develops and cultivates the five powers slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.
translation_text.sn50.55-66:1.3: And how does a mendicant who develops the five powers slant, slope, and incline to extinguishment?
translation_text.sn50.55-66:1.4: It’s when a mendicant develops the powers of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom, which culminate in the removal of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.sn50.55-66:1.5: That’s how a mendicant who develops and cultivates the five powers slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.”
translation_text.sn50.55-66:1.6: (Tell in full for each of the different rivers as in SN 45.91–102, removal of greed version.)
translation_text.sn50.55-66:2.1: Six on slanting to the east,
translation_text.sn50.55-66:2.2: and six on slanting to the ocean;
translation_text.sn50.55-66:2.4: and that’s how this chapter is recited.
